title: NTT Plans $10 Billion Bond in Biggest From Asian Firm This Year
summary: Japanese telecom giant NTT Inc. is planning to raise about $10 billion in a multi-currency debt offering, people familiar with the matter said, in what would be the largest global bond sale by an Asian company so far this year.

title: BlackRock-Backed AIP, KKR Said to Court Stack Asia Data Centers
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Partnership and Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. are among possible bidders for Stack Infrastructure Inc.’s data centers in the Asia-Pacific region, according to people familiar with the matter.

summary: As Russia’s war in Ukraine drags on, and spills into the rest of Europe, outside interest in the Finnish by-invitation-only training is growing.

title: Oil Tanker Booked in Persian Gulf at 897% of Benchmark Rate
summary: One of the world’s biggest operators of supertankers has provisionally booked a vessel to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at nearly nine times benchmark rates, a price that reflects the shortage of avalable ships in the area.

title: CATL Sees Its Sodium Batteries in at Least 10,000 EVs This Year
summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. expects 10,000 to 20,000 electric vehicles to carry its sodium-ion batteries this year, a senior official for the Chinese company said.

title: Hedge Funds Look Set to Win Another Exemption From EU ESG Rules
summary: Hedge funds are set to be excluded from a requirement that would force investment managers in Europe to categorize whether their products are ESG or not.

title: Stock Rout Pauses as Traders Await Micron Earnings: Markets Wrap
summary: A measure of calm returned to equity markets after a selloff in top-performing technology names, with Micron Technology Inc.’s earnings taking on added significance as a test for the artificial intelligence trade.

title: Micron Earnings Take on New Gravity With Market on Edge Over AI
summary: Micron Technology Inc.’s earnings report on Wednesday afternoon is shaping up to be one of the most important in months as investors find themselves suddenly on edge over the sustainability of the AI rally.

title: Rheinmetall Tumbles as Germany Said to Shelve Warships Contract
summary: Rheinmetall AG shares fell the most in a year on concern the defense firm will come under pressure as Germany plans to withdraw a major warships contract.

title: Tankmaker KNDS Starts Process for Frankfurt, Paris IPO
summary: Tankmaker KNDS started the process to hold an initial public offering in Frankfurt and Paris on Wednesday, a step forward in its development as a pan-European defense company. Bloomberg's Charles Capel reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Says Iran Crisis Shows Nations Must Speed Up Energy Shift
summary: Oil and gas shortages triggered by the US war against Iran should be a lesson to countries to accelerate their energy transitions, according to China’s climate envoy.

title: Vedanta Eyes $372 Million Raise From Zambia Copper Mines IPO
summary: Vedanta Resources Ltd. plans to sell an 11.9% stake in a US-based unit to raise about $372 million to develop its Zambian copper mining complex.

title: US Diesel Prices Fall Under $5, Easing Inflationary Pressure
summary: The cost of diesel in the US fell below $5 a gallon for the first time since mid-March, offering some relief for one of the global economy’s most important fuels.

title: Xi Pressures Takaichi by Throttling Key Mineral Exports to Japan
summary: China is choking shipments of some critical minerals to Japan, a slowdown that’s hurting companies and prompting calls for Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to find a diplomatic off-ramp with Beijing.

title: Tankmaker KNDS Kicks Off Process for Frankfurt and Paris IPO
summary: Tankmaker KNDS NV started the process to hold an initial public offering in Frankfurt and Paris in the next few weeks as it moves forward with plans to become a pan-European defense company.

title: India Offers Leverage on Forex Deposits to Boost Inflows
summary: India’s central bank allowed lenders to extend loans against foreign-currency deposits raised under a special program offered to overseas citizens, a move that could see billions in dollar inflows over the next few months.

title: Airbus A380 Aircraft Require Checks for Possible Wing Cracks
summary: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency is asking some operators of Airbus SE A380 jets to perform additional special inspections on their aircraft’s wings after identifying possible cracks in the structure.

title: Volatility Is Now a Feature, Not a Bug: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BOJ’s Ueda Reiterates Risk of Inflation Exceeding 2% Target
summary: The Bank of Japan sees the risk of inflation exceeding its 2% target and will conduct additional interest-rate hikes appropriately, Governor Kazuo Ueda said in speech Wednesday that reiterated policymakers’ recent messaging.

title: Adani Bets on India Energy Security With Nuclear Power Project
summary: Billionaire Gautam Adani said his giant conglomerate will embark on a new 10 gigawatt nuclear power initiative to help improve India’s energy security, in his first public speech since resolving his legal troubles in the US.

title: Asia’s Demand for Middle Eastern Oil Slows After Buying Spree
summary: Asian refiners have slowed purchases of Middle Eastern crude after a buying spree over the past three weeks, with oil majors and traders stepping in to take some of the surplus barrels.

title: Gold Drops Below $4,100 as Stronger Dollar, Rate Outlook Weigh
summary: Gold fell for a second day, under pressure from a stronger US dollar and a tech-led selloff in equities that’s prompted investors to cut bullion holdings and cover losses elsewhere.

title: Tech Stocks Set to Bounce After $1.3 Trillion Rout on AI Jitters
summary: US technology stocks were set to rebound on Wednesday after concerns around artificial intelligence valuations wiped out nearly $1.3 trillion from the market capitalization of Nasdaq 100 stocks over two days.

title: Chinese Premier Says Tech Industry Not a Global Threat | The China Show 6/24/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tuya Sees Growth Despite China Demand Slowdown
summary: Tuya Smart, an AI cloud and IoT platform provider, says growth remains strong despite slowing consumer appliance demand in China. Co-founder and COO, Alex Young, joined Bloomberg's Minmin Low on the sidelines of the World Economic Forum in Dalian and appeared on Insight with Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: PBOC Adviser Sees Potential Rate Cut
summary: Huang Yiping, a member of the monetary policy committee at the People’s Bank of China, says he sees a potential rate cut this year. He joined Bloomberg's Minmin Low on the sidelines of the World Economic Forum in Dalian and appeared on Insight with Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 6/24/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: McKinsey’s Ngai on China's 'Opportunity 2.0'
summary: McKinsey Greater China Chairman, Joe Ngai, says China's “Opportunity 2.0” narrative is gaining prominence as innovation, consumption, and advanced manufacturing reshape its growth outlook. He joined Bloomberg's Minmin Low on the sidelines of the World Economic Forum in Dalian and appeared on Insight with Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Temasek Unit, Oman Fund Join Vinpearl’s Private Credit Deal
summary: A unit of Singapore’s Temasek Holdings Pte. and Oman’s sovereign wealth fund are investing in a $255 million private credit financing for Vingroup JSC’s hospitality arm, underscoring continued appetite among state-backed investors in such opportunities in Asia.

title: RBA Cites Phillips Curve in Bet Its Rate Hikes Won’t Hammer Jobs
summary: Australia’s central bank is betting the interaction between prices and jobs along the Phillips Curve mean its rapid-fire interest rate hikes will damp inflation without pushing unemployment too high, according to Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser.

title: Heat Wave Hits Europe, Causing Disruption
summary: Extreme weather will continue across northwest Europe through the weekend, testing temperature records, according to weather models and analysis from meteorologists. London is forecast to breach 35C on Wednesday. Bloomberg's Joe Wertz reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Beijing Unfazed by China Shock 2.0 as Premier Touts Openness
summary: Beijing’s No. 2 official played down worries about the disruption wrought by his country’s surging exports on the global economy, instead championing the benefits provided to the world by China’s development.
